steps.title: Compare spread from the mean
steps.content: Standard deviation measures how spread out the data values are from their mean. Values clustered close to the mean have a smaller standard deviation, while values spread farther away have a larger standard deviation. The means identify the center of each histogram, not the amount of spread by themselves.

steps.title: Read group A
steps.content: Group A has more weight farther from its mean of $2.1$. Its histogram places substantial frequency near the outer weights, so many objects are not close to the center. That wider spread makes its standard deviation larger.

steps.title: Read group B
steps.content: Group B is more tightly clustered near its mean of $11.1$. Most objects are close to the center of that distribution, with less frequency far out in the tails. A tighter cluster means a smaller standard deviation.

steps.title: Choose the larger spread
steps.content: Because group A is more spread out from its mean than group B, group A has the greater standard deviation. The answer is C.
